MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. JOHN H. RUSSELL, Esq. of Law Office of John H. Russell (Tel: 415-394-1098) for plaintiff;
Atty. CHRISTOPHER B. DALEY, Esq. of Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt, LLP (Tel: 415-278-9400) for defendants Kaufoou Fakava and Joel Fakava.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JOEL FAKAVA AND KAUFOOU FAKAVA'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

The Court notes it did not reeceive a notice of contest from defense counsel but permits argument from counsel for parties.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to stay this civil action pending final disposition of a related criminal action is granted.

Joel Fakava is charged with felonies for motor vehicle collisions in 2023; Kaufoou Fakava owned the vehicle Joel allegedly drove. When defendants face criminal and civil jeopardy at the same time, the civil case is commonly stayed pending disposition of the criminal case, for self-incrimination reasons. (See Pacers, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 686, 690.)

Plaintiff concedes the above, but argues for what he deems non-incriminatory civil discovery while the criminal case is ongoing. However, that would be a recipe for time-wasting disputes and line-drawing issues.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
